calendar.matters:
Petition For Aproval Of Settlement Agreements Relating To Beneficial Interests Of Regina Hagan And Bernard Hagan, Jr. In The Bernard P. Hagan Trust And The I Trust
Off Calendar Oct-16-2023 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PTR-17-301371 In re: Bernard P. Hagan and
PTR-21-304383 In re: Roseanne Bertucelli as Trustee of The I Trust

Appearances: Jeffrey Loew appeared by zoom for Will Monroe (beneficiary).
Jessica Neugebauer appeared by zoom for Bernard Hagan (beneficiary).
Gabrielle Korte appeared by zoom for Regina Hagan (beneficiary).
Robert Cross appeared by zoom for Paula Bennett, Maryann Bertucelli and Peter Renstrom (respondents).

The court noted that the settlement agreement has language in it about how it is going to become "null and void" if not approved by the court by a certain date, and that the date has passed. Mr. Cross stated that they had several continuances, that the settling parties have agreed to waive court approval, that they are working on it now, that Will's share was distributed by the trustee, and that they are still hoping to speak with Judge Sugiyama. Mr. Cross also stated that dismissals by Mr. Monroe have not been received as of yet and asked the court to take the matters off calendar as moot. Mr. Loew stated that he believes his client is willing to sign the dismissals. No one objects to the court taking the matters off calendar as moot. The Petitions for Approval of Settlement Agreements (2), and the Petition for Instructions Re: Distribution, etc. are off calendar as moot.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

Petition (1) To Invalidate Restatement Dated October 19, 2016 Based On Undue Influence; (2) For Transfer Of Trust Property Pursuant To Probate Code 850 Et Seq.; (3) For Financial Elder Abuse; And (4) In The Alternative, Intentional Interference With Expected Inheritance
Off Calendar Jun-01-2022 Continued To Jun-15-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Byron Sun, via Zoom, for Kelly Harlan, Petitioner; Robert Cross and Alex Weingarten, via Zoom, for Paula Bennett, Objector.

The court queries Mr. Sun regarding Kelly Harlan's search for a new attorney. Mr. Sun reports that Ms. Harlan has instructed him to file a Request for a Dismissal of her petition. Mr. Sun reports of being contacted by another beneficiary named Bernard Lee Hagan who advised that he considered joining the matter and making an appearance today to advise the court of his objection. The court notes that the person that Mr. Sun is referring to did not make an appearance today. Mr. Weingarten makes an oral request for the court to dismiss Kelly Harlan's petition on the record. The court denies Mr. Weingarten's request advising that it would prefer the Request for Dismissal formally filed. The court instructs Mr. Sun to forward a filed copy of the Request for Dismissal to the assigned Examiner via email.

The Petition to Invalidate Restatement dated Oct-19-2016, etc., is continued to Jun-15-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Martinez. Not Reported.

Petition For Order Determining Interest In Propety And Directing Respondent Bernard J Hagan To Transfer Possession To Trustee Paula Hagan Bennett
Off Calendar Oct-09-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Neil Moran for Paula Hagan Bennett (Trustee).
Aaron Mohamed for Bernard Hagan (Respondent/Objector).

The Court stated that it read the briefs, that the issue is whether it is appropriate here (Probate) when title is not an issue and that this is a get around in an unlawful detainer. Mr. Moran stated that the opposition makes no mention of 850 and argued that Probate Code 850 confers the Probate Court jurisdiction. The Court denied the Petition for Order Determining Interest in Property, etc. stating that this is not the proper forum for the issue that is actually in dispute. Mr. Mohamed is to provide a form of order.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition For Order Determining Interest In Propety And Directing Respondent Bernard J Hagan To Transfer Possession To Trustee Paula Hagan Bennett
Off Calendar Sep-18-2018 Continued To Oct-09-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Neil Moran for Paula Bennett (Petitioner).
Paul Peterson for Bernard J. Hagan.

The Court noted that there is no dispute as to whether this property belongs to the Trust and raised the issue of this being a civil matter. Mr. Peterson agrees that there is no dispute that the property should be title to the Trust. Mr. Moran stated that this is properly brought under Probate Code 850(a)(3)(A), that the Trust holds title to the property, that the Respondent claims a right to possession and that the Court has jurisdiction under Probate Codes 859 and 851. The Court informed Mr. Moran that it will not evict someone, that eviction is not done in the Probate Court, that it is apparent to the Court that this doesn't appear to belong in Probate and that he can further brief it if he chooses to. Mr. Moran stated that he would like to speak with his Client. The Petition for Order Determining Interest in Property, etc. is continued to October 9, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Moran is to file his pleadings to support that the case belongs in probate, if any, no later than October 1, 2018. Mr. Peterson is to file responsive pleadings, if any, by October 3, 2018.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition For Approval Of Creditors Claims
Off Calendar Mar-06-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Alexandria Letzel for Paula Hagan Bennett (Successor Trustee)

Ms. Letzel addressed the issue of venue, stating that trust documents, books, statements and records are located in an office/apartment in San Francisco, and that the Trust is administered here. Ms. Letzel also stated that the Trustee has allowed the creditors claims, but is requesting Court approval of the allowance of those claims, that the grandchildren (ages 14 and 17) are purely discretionary and contingent beneficiaries, that their interests are not effected by this petition and therefore notice is not required to be given. In addition, Ms. Letzel stated that she believes the grandchildren have received a copy of the Trust and the Trustee is making sure their higher education needs will be taken care of. The Petition for Approval of Creditors Claims is Taken Under Submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le, Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.
